▼

Amazon Nova Act Action Viewer

▼ Prompt
You are performing a browser evidence capture demo. Use only the URLs that are explicitly provided in this prompt. Summarize visible facts only. Do not search for a person by name on LinkedIn or Google. Do not make personal judgments, and do not contact anyone. Only inspect information already visible in the current browser session. Narrate actions briefly while moving. Scroll only when needed. At most 2 scroll passes per section. If you overscroll, use direct navigation, page anchors, or return to the previous visible section instead of endless scrolling. Candidate label for notes: Nguyen Phan Nguyen. LINKEDIN STAGE: COMPLETE THESE 30 EVIDENCE-CAPTURE STEPS L1. The human operator already signed in manually before this stage starts. L2. If you are not already on the provided LinkedIn URL, navigate directly to it now: https://www.linkedin.com/in/nguyenpn1/ L3. Stay on the provided LinkedIn profile only. Do not search LinkedIn for people by name. L4. Wait for the profile page to load completely. L5. If LinkedIn redirects to /login, an authwall, or 'Sign in to see more' and no profile content is visible, set LINKEDIN_STATUS: blocked. L6. If blocked, do not attempt bypass and stop the LinkedIn stage. L7. If the profile is visible, set LINKEDIN_STATUS: ok. L8. Capture the visible profile URL. L9. Capture the visible top-card headline exactly as shown. L10. Capture the visible location exactly as shown. L11. Capture the visible current company or current role line from the top card. L12. Capture the visible school or education summary from the top card. L13. Capture any visible public links or portfolio links in the top card or contact/public section. L14. Note whether About, Featured, Activity, Experience, Education, Skills, or Certifications sections are visible. L15. If an About section is visible, focus it and capture a short visible summary only. L16. Capture any clearly stated technical interests, specialties, or themes from the About section. L17. Scroll toward the Featured or Activity section if visible. L18. If Featured items are visible, capture the titles and what type of item each appears to be. L19. If Activity is visible, capture whether posts, reposts, talks, or project updates are visible. L20. Scroll to the Experience section. L21. Capture the most recent visible role title. L22. Capture the most recent visible company name and date range. L23. Capture one additional relevant visible role, internship, or research position if shown. L24. Capture any visible impact statements, technologies, or responsibilities that are explicitly written. L25. Scroll to the Education section. L26. Capture visible school name, degree, field, and dates. L27. Capture visible honors, societies, or organizations only if explicitly shown near education. L28. Scroll to the Skills section. L29. Capture only the first 5 visible skills exactly as labeled on the current page. Do not click 'Show all skills' and do not remain on a /details/skills page. L30. End the LinkedIn stage immediately after top card, About, Experience, Education, and first 5 visible skills are captured. FINAL REPORT: F1. After the LinkedIn evidence is captured or blocked, call return() immediately for this stage. F2. The final return() must be plain text and include these lines exactly: F3. LINKEDIN_STATUS: ok/blocked/skipped F4. SUMMARY: F5. - 4 to 8 bullets of visible LinkedIn facts only such as headline, location, company, education, visible links, featured items, roles, dates, and top visible skills. F6. Do not include GITHUB_STATUS or WEB_STATUS in this stage return. F7. Do not include judgments or claims beyond visible page facts.
